rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by fixing unresolved references or removing invalid guarantees
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Align helper declaration types with expression types (bool for comparisons, int for integer arithmetic, real for real arithmetic)
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool in const and eq declarations
generic_rules: Preserve existing architecture when repairing validator diagnostics
Remove invalid guarantees instead of adding architecture when references are unresolved
Use only visible identifiers in the current scope for references
Align data types in helper declarations with the expressions they support
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool
Comparison expressions should be of type bool
Integer arithmetic should use int type
Real arithmetic should use real type
anti_patterns: Do not use package-qualified identifiers in generated AGREE code
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not introduce data types or property references in generated AGREE code that are absent from the analyzed model and its configured dependencies
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and matching with clauses
AGREE assumptions and guarantees placement in component type scopes
AGREE contracts written inside annex agree blocks
Component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
AGREE code patterns for input validity and range monitoring
AGREE data type declarations and extensions
